item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know it's a little old now but one thing I think is missing is that they would have done genetic testing on it. Probably would show it was human or doesn't match closely with anything on record (due to extensive modification) if you wanted to keep it ambiguous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. Interesting, but trite and uncompelling. -1

(I did like the implication that it ate its captors.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
rod surgically implanted within the [REDACTED] of the specimen.

*snicker*

Anyway, the addendums are almost asking for the SCP to be a part of some GOI. There's also a small typo in the containment procedures (puréed).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with thedeadlymoose's appraisal. It's… okay. Not bad, not great, just okay. It doesn't do anything to really stand out. It's skirting the line between Euclid and Safe, though I'm leaning towards Safe, as it hasn't attacked maintenance personnel and containing it doesn't seem to be a huge issue.

And one final nitpick: "Entity" is more of a Global Occult Coalition term. To each his own, but my headcanon is that each organization will try to avoid using one-another's terminology… for many reasons involving internal affairs, and not just because a certain other SCP will freak the hell out when it hears GOC terms.

item.title: Downvoted for lack of an apostrophe.
(no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's… it's alright. Neither really bad nor really good.

No vote for now.

EDIT: On second read, downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feel free, I left the description a little vague so that it would be open to interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first pictured it, I envisioned the bastard offspring of a human and a deep-sea tube worm. I'm sticking to that interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So , PM'd you two.. we need to discuss this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reads better than before. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was definitely not Prometheus Labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made an update to the text, does it work better now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It gives a little glimpse into the past of the thing, just enough to give you an idea of where it came from, but not giving away everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I'm glad this is doing well, but I want to improve it, I want it to have a hook like people are mentioning. I'm open to suggestions on improvements that can be made.

I also wrote up a bit of 'additional' documentation that might help but I don't want to it if it would mess things up.

Additional documentation was recovered from a USB flash drive located within the subjects torso as well as several scraps of white cloth, recovered data was heavily encrypted and the device was partially damaged from acid exposure. The following document seems to outline the subjects initial capture and experimentation:

Series: 0021c

Date: March 15, 1981
Location: █████ ███████, Saskatchewan, Canada

Subject: ███████ █████████

Capture Report: Squad A53-b located the subject at approximately 0700 - Sunday, no reported casualties. Subject successfully incapacitated before being relocated to Test Facility DBO-18c-2.

Chemical Exposure Report: Subject A-35 remained fully submerged for 23 hours, upon removal from Vat 17-GH8-1b subject began losing cohesion between the hypodermis and underlying muscle tissue, subject was subsequently sedated.

Initial Surgical Report: Subject A-35 was moved to Surgical Bay E-2a for additional chemical exposure bef-

All remaining information was too heavily corrupted for proper recovery, further investigation into the organization behind SCP-1542s creation is currently underway.

(I know Vae mentioned a section title but I'm not sure what he meant by that)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact: I was very nervous about posting this because I didn't want it to seem like I was copying the idea (human what was not human.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm in with the "it needs a hook" crowd. It's not bad (though it needs more apostrophes), but there's really nothing going on here. It's just a thing what is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it meant to sound like there's a pipe shoved up its ass?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I read the inscription on the rod, I got the picture that Promethious Labs might've done it. We've really only seen machines from them, but who says they haven't done a little genetic engineering?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this feels like half an article. it's a very good half, but it's still missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mad, unnatural and complex.
I love it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell yeah! Add in some kind of serial number tattooed on the skin, or an implant similar to this one, and we have ourselves a series. This might be the thing to lure me out of my current lethargic procrastination and write another SCP. I normally avoid emoticons, but I'm going to make an exception. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes me think… the Hungarian Floater rewrite I slapped up recently is also an odd humanflesh creature. Potential linkage ? =^.^=

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, and I'd certainly enjoy a series of these. Modifying humans in such a manner doesn't fit the MO of any GoI I can think of, except maybe Wondertainment, but this guy is no Little Mister. There is a great opportunity here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eww. Yuck.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is written to norms, the creature is original, but the subject is still boring. No scare or twist or intrigue or cool or funny.

Has potential, but I'd say it needs another pass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Close, but more like this except made of exposed muscle coated in a viscous slime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A title of the section?

Well the thing was meant for sale before they scrapped the project and literally flushed the subject, I'm not sure where else the information could be located since the acids would've dissolved any paper products that were flushed with the entity. Perhaps a secondary metal insert or a tracking chip of some sort embedded in the subjects, uhm, brain area?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting. The specific subject being located, as well as the "no reported casualties" suggests they were looking for someone specific, and somewhat dangerous.

Something about the purpose of this, perhaps.. maybe a title of the section.

That said ,a bit of fridge logic - why would recovery data be on the thing itself? If you want this sort of stuff, have them find it somewhere related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://pastebin.com/AWUTqcST

Opinions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This might help http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ElI7IiJTXp0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, I'll start work on the additional recovered documentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's meant that the nerves can do what the muscles do so they don't get torn when the subject stretches or collapses as it moves through out the pipes and stuff. Is there a way I can improve that or make it more clear?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually know what it looks like now… sorta… he found his hole right?
Fluttershy: "I wish I was a tree." *shudder*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure. Better have it implied by some sort of supplementary document.
As I said… documents from its creators would help a whole lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
inside the torso, and a heavily modified nervous system capable of expanding and contorting in response to movement has been weaved throughout the skeletal structure

I don't think the nervous system can do that, rather than regulating the muscles which do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, doing it that way would be too obvious, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That wouldn't be incorrect, but as Scordatura mentioned it should look more like an Amigara Fault monster without skin or defining characteristics (no fingers, toes, elbows, knees, face, ribs, hips.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I originally had it specifically mentioned that the thing was "X Y reported missing from A, B in 1981" but was removed on advice from the forum. Would adding it back in help at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well.. given this sits at -1, your friends were probably wrong.

Also, I see this as a novel way of sewage treatment.

Also, why am I picturing a giant dick, especially knowing you wrote it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, but you don't have it come across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually both, to an extent, somebody out there took a human off the streets and heavily modified him to function as designed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm picturing one of the things from The Enigma of Amigara Fault living in a sewer.

I'm actually pretty okay with that image. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is written to norms, the creature is original, but the subject is still boring. No scare or twist or intrigue or cool or funny. No vote for now.

I suggest trying to work in some kind of hook. To me, you already got two you could expand upon, just pick one:

1) Something or someone out there is manufacturing design organisms and a specimen found itself in the wilds. Who, how or why?

2) A sapient mind intelligent enough to try to communicate (or so the article assumes) is stuck in the body of what I can only picture as a Legend of Zelda Like-Like (the tubes that eat your shield). How does it feel? It must be incredibly frustrated or depressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I'm admittedly hesitant to ask for help so directly as I don't want to seem a burden. Next time I'll be sure to give you a tap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't really fit the clinical tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thus my confusion, thank you for the bit of humour though. :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting… in the future, if you have a draft, feel free to PM me a link. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Under most circumstances "cocktail" is fine… but then you have Dr. Bright hanging around…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was posted up on the forums for about a month now, that's where I heard cocktail was a fine word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Solution" might work better.

Also, in the future, it's better to get more feedback on the forums instead of cold posting with glaring problems like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I looked for a picture but I wasn't able to find any that worked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was told cocktail was fine, do you have a suggestion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not exactly sure what I'm looking at here… maybe some more descriptive text of the entity or a picture might help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I initially had the rod containing a microchip that would have schematics and documents on the subject but my friends felt it was unnecessary.

I'm having a difficult time finding the correct technical term for slithering (Wikipedia notes that slithering isn't the correct technical term but doesn't list what that term is.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still a couple small issues (you used the word cocktail…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, changed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs some fixing-up and expanding upon…. but I actually kinda like it. (Better tone for one .. stuff like 'hide' , slithers around, and theoretically ensnaring prey is just silly.)
Also, tell us more about why it was made.. perhaps the label being more descriptive.

Tentative upvote, hoping it'll get fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does have human intelligence but it has no way to communicate higher intelligence. I felt it best to tag it as sentient since there is no way for the subject to display it's sapience (it can't speak, it has no fingers, it's covered in an acidic slime so it can't manipulate inks/paints.)
